300f || currentSize.x == 0)\r\n                    {\r\n                        rectTransform.sizeDelta = new Vector2(300f, currentSize.y);\r\n                    }\r\n                }\r\n            }\r\n            catch\r\n            {\r\n                // Silently ignore errors\r\n            }\r\n        }\r\n\r\n        /// &lt;summary&gt;\r\n        /// Adds tooltip components to Prefab Name and Prefab Hash text elements.\r\n        /// Tooltips show full value, click-to-copy instruction, and IC10 usage info.\r\n        /// &lt;/summary&gt;\r\n        private static void AddPrefabTooltips(UniversalPage page)\r\n        {\r\n            try\r\n            {\r\n                string pageKey = Stationpedia.Instance?.CurrentPage?.Key;\r\n                \r\n                // Add tooltip to Prefab Name\r\n                if (page.PrefabNameText != null &amp;&amp; !string.IsNullOrEmpty(page.PrefabHashText.text))\r\n                {\r\n                    var prefabHashParent = page.PrefabHashText.transform.parent;\r\n                    if (prefabHashParent != null)\r\n                    {\r\n                        // Get or add tooltip component to the parent (the row containing label + value)\r\n                        var tooltip = prefabHashParent.gameObject.GetComponent&lt;Tooltips.SPDAPrefabInfoTooltip&gt;();\r\n                        if (tooltip == null)\r\n                        {\r\n                            tooltip = prefabHashParent.gameObject.AddComponent&lt;Tooltips.SPDAPrefabInfoTooltip&gt;();\r\n                        }\r\n                        \r\n                        // Extract clean prefab hash (remove link formatting)\r\n                        string cleanHash = ExtractPrefabValue(page.PrefabHashText.text);\r\n                        tooltip.Initialize(pageKey ?? \"\", cleanHash, true);\r\n                    }\r\n                }\r\n            }\r\n            catch\r\n            {\r\n                // Silently ignore errors\r\n            }\r\n        }\r\n\r\n        /// &lt;summary&gt;\r\n        /// Extracts the actual value from TMP link-formatted text.\r\n        /// Input like: &lt;link=Clipboard&gt;&lt;color=#008AE6&gt;StructurePressureFedLiquidRocketEngine&lt;/color&gt;&lt;/link&gt;\r\n        /// Output: StructurePressureFedLiquidRocketEngine\r\n        /// &lt;/summary&gt;\r\n        private static string ExtractPrefabValue(string formattedText)\r\n        {\r\n            if (string.IsNullOrEmpty(formattedText))\r\n                return formattedText;\r\n            \r\n            // Remove all tags\r\n            string result = System.Text.RegularExpressions.Regex.Replace(formattedText, \"&lt;[^&gt;]+&gt;\", \"\");\r\n            return result.Trim();",
```json
  "toolCallResult": null
}
```

The user wants me to:
1. Make the prefab name truncation smart - only truncate if there's something to the right (like Stack Size)
2. Add tooltips to both Prefab Name and Prefab Hash that:
   - Show full name/hash
   - Say "Click to copy"
   - Explain what they're used for (IC10 coding)

Let me first look at the game code to understand the UniversalPage structure and what fields are available.Let me search for the UniversalPage class to understand the layout and available fields: Now I understand the structure. I can see that:
1. `PrefabNameText` and `PrefabHashText` are TextMeshProUGUI elements
2. `StackSizeInfo` is on the same row (in `GeneralInfoPanel`)
3. The game already makes these clickable with link type "Clipboard"

Let me look at how tooltips are added for other elements in your mod: Now let me create a new tooltip class for prefab info and update the `Patches.cs` with the smart truncation logic and tooltip attachment. First, let me create the new tooltip class: Now let me read the current `Patches.cs` to see where to add the `AddPrefabTooltips` method and update the truncation logic: Now let me update the `Patches.cs` with the smart truncation logic and add the `AddPrefabTooltips` method: Now let's build the project to make sure everything compiles: Let me check what the correct property is in the Stationpedia class: I see - it's `Stationpedia.CurrentPageKey` (static), not `CurrentPage.Key`. Let me check the current `Patches.cs` to see the exact context and fix the issue: I see the issue. At line 218, I have `Stationpedia.Instance?.CurrentPage?.Key` but it should be `Stationpedia.CurrentPageKey` (it's a static property). Let me also check the SPDAPrefabInfoTooltip to see its current state: Now let me fix the `Stationpedia.Instance?.CurrentPage?.Key` error by replacing it with the static `Stationpedia.CurrentPageKey`: Now let me check if `CleanName` is defined in the base class: Good, `CleanName` is a protected static method in the base class. Now let's build and see if there are any other errors: The build succeeded. Let me now deploy it to the game directory for testing: The mod has been built and deployed. Here's a summary of what was implemented:

## Summary of Changes

### 1. Smart Truncation for Prefab Names
In `undefined`, the `TruncateLongPrefabName()` method now:
- Checks if `StackSizeInfo` is visible (active and has text)
- **Only applies truncation** when there's something on the right side that it could overlap with
- If no Stack Size is shown, resets the text field to allow full width display

### 2. Prefab Info Tooltips with IC10 Examples
Created `SPDAPrefabInfoTooltip.cs` which provides:
- Full prefab name/hash value (in case truncated)
- "**Click to copy**" instruction (green text)
- Explanation that prefab name and hash are used for IC10 programming
- Note that they are interchangeable
- Example IC10 code snippet showing usage: `lb r0 <value> Setting`

### 3. Tooltip Attachment
The `AddPrefabTooltips()` method in `undefined`:
- Attaches tooltips to both Prefab Name and Prefab Hash parent elements
- Cleans link formatting to extract raw values
- Uses `Stationpedia.CurrentPageKey` (static property) for the page key

**Fixed Bug:** Changed `Stationpedia.Instance?.CurrentPage?.Key` → `Stationpedia.CurrentPageKey`

You can now test it in-game! The tooltips will appear when you hover over the "Prefab Name:" or "Prefab Hash:" rows in the Stationpedia.

digits to display object names\r\n    --src-prefix &lt;prefix&gt; show the given source prefix instead of \"a/\"\r\n    --dst-prefix &lt;prefix&gt; show the given destination prefix instead of \"b/\"     \r\n    --line-prefix &lt;prefix&gt;\r\n                          prepend an additional prefix to every line of output  \r\n    --no-prefix           do not show any source or destination prefix\r\n    --default-prefix      use default prefixes a/ and b/\r\n    --inter-hunk-context &lt;n&gt;\r\n                          show context between diff hunks up to the specified number of lines\r\n    --output-indicator-new &lt;char&gt;\r\n                          specify the character to indicate a new line instead of '+'\r\n    --output-indicator-old &lt;char&gt;\r\n                          specify the character to indicate an old line instead of '-'\r\n    --output-indicator-context &lt;char&gt;\r\n                          specify the character to indicate a context instead of ' '\r\n\r\nDiff rename options\r\n    -B, --break-rewrites[=&lt;n&gt;[/&lt;m&gt;]]\r\n                          break complete rewrite changes into pairs of delete and create\r\n    -M, --find-renames[=&lt;n&gt;]\r\n                          detect renames\r\n    -D, --irreversible-delete\r\n                          omit the preimage for deletes\r\n    -C, --find-copies[=&lt;n&gt;]\r\n                          detect copies\r\n    --[no-]find-copies-harder\r\n                          use unmodified files as source to find copies\r\n    --no-renames          disable rename detection\r\n    --[no-]rename-empty   use empty blobs as rename source\r\n    --[no-]follow         continue listing the history of a file beyond renames \r\n    -l &lt;n&gt;   "content": "&#96;&#96;&#96;markdown\n2] - 2025-12-30\r\n\r\n### ⚡ Search Performance Optimizations\r\n- **O(1) Title Index Lookups** - Built cached title/word indexes for instant lookups instead of O(n) full scans\r\n- **Reduced Search Latency** - Replaced 1-second polling loop with 2-frame delay for near-instant results\r\n- **Category Cache** - Added category lookup cache to avoid triple-nested loops during reorganization\r\n- **Template Caching** - Cached font, sprite, and color references to avoid repeated lookups\r\n\r\n### 🏷️ New Property Tooltips\r\nHover over gas, material, and device properties to see detailed explanations:\r\n- **Gas/Material Properties**: Flashpoint, Autoignition, Thermal Convection, Thermal Radiation, Solar Heating, Specific Heat, Freeze Temperature, Boiling Temperature, Max Liquid Temperature, Min Liquid Pressure, Latent Heat, Moles Per Litre\r\n- **Device Properties**: Max Pressure, Volume, Base Power, Power Storage, Power Generation\r\n- **Plant/Food Properties**: Growth Time, Nutrition, Nutrition Quality, Mood Bonus\r\n- **Rocket Properties**: Placeable In Rocket, Rocket Mass\r\n\r\n### 🎮 IC10 Helper Tooltips\r\n- **Prefab Name Tooltip** - Shows full name (in case truncated), \"click to copy\" instruction, and IC10 usage example\r\n- **Prefab Hash Tooltip** - Shows hash value with IC10 code example (&#96;lb r0 &lt;hash&gt; Setting&#96;)\r\n- Explains that Prefab Name and Hash are interchangeable for IC10 programming\r\n\r\n### 🔧 Bug Fixes &amp; Polish\r\n- **Prefab Name Truncation** - Fixed truncation to prevent overlap with Stack Size field\r\n- **Removed Debug Spam** - Cleaned up console logs by removing verbose debug output\r\n- **New Tooltip Classes** - Added &#96;SPDAPrefabInfoTooltip&#96; and &#96;SPDAPropertyTooltip&#96;\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n## [0.2.\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n",
```json
  "textEditContent": "2] - 2025-12-30\r\n\r\n### ⚡ Search Performance Optimizations\r\n- **O(1) Title Index Lookups** - Built cached title/word indexes for instant lookups instead of O(n) full scans\r\n- **Reduced Search Latency** - Replaced 1-second polling loop with 2-frame delay for near-instant results\r\n- **Category Cache** - Added category lookup cache to avoid triple-nested loops during reorganization\r\n- **Template Caching** - Cached font, sprite, and color references to avoid repeated lookups\r\n\r\n### 🏷️ New Property Tooltips\r\nHover over gas, material, and device properties to see detailed explanations:\r\n- **Gas/Material Properties**: Flashpoint, Autoignition, Thermal Convection, Thermal Radiation, Solar Heating, Specific Heat, Freeze Temperature, Boiling Temperature, Max Liquid Temperature, Min Liquid Pressure, Latent Heat, Moles Per Litre\r\n- **Device Properties**: Max Pressure, Volume, Base Power, Power Storage, Power Generation\r\n- **Plant/Food Properties**: Growth Time, Nutrition, Nutrition Quality, Mood Bonus\r\n- **Rocket Properties**: Placeable In Rocket, Rocket Mass\r\n\r\n### 🎮 IC10 Helper Tooltips\r\n- **Prefab Name Tooltip** - Shows full name (in case truncated), \"click to copy\" instruction, and IC10 usage example\r\n- **Prefab Hash Tooltip** - Shows hash value with IC10 code example (&#96;lb r0 &lt;hash&gt; Setting&#96;)\r\n- Explains that Prefab Name and Hash are interchangeable for IC10 programming\r\n\r\n### 🔧 Bug Fixes &amp; Polish\r\n- **Prefab Name Truncation** - Fixed truncation to prevent overlap with Stack Size field\r\n- **Removed Debug Spam** - Cleaned up console logs by removing verbose debug output\r\n- **New Tooltip Classes** - Added &#96;SPDAPrefabInfoTooltip&#96; and &#96;SPDAPropertyTooltip&#96;\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n## [0.2.",
```

I noticed in the git commit there were files like `workshop_update.vdf` and `workshop_update_no_preview.vdf` - these are the Steam Workshop item configuration files.

Let me check what they have and how the current release build task is set up, then modify the task to include the SteamCMD upload.Yes! Let me check your current setup and VDF files: I can see you already have a `workshop_update.vdf` file. Now let me check if SteamCMD is installed and update your release task: SteamCMD is at `steamcmd.exe`. Now I'll update the release task and VDF file: Now you need to set up your Steam credentials. For security, we should use an environment variable or a saved login. Let me check if you have a cached SteamCMD login: You have saved credentials. Let me update the task to use them properly: Done! Here's what I set up:

**Three new tasks available:**

1. **`Package StationpediaAscended (Release)`** - Builds the release (unchanged)

2. **`Publish to Steam Workshop`** - Uploads to Workshop using SteamCMD with your saved credentials

3. **`Release & Publish StationpediaAscended`** - Runs both in sequence (build → publish)

**To use:**
- Press `Ctrl+Shift+P` → "Tasks: Run Task" → **"Release & Publish StationpediaAscended"**

**Note:** The first time you run it, SteamCMD may prompt for Steam Guard if your session expired. After that, it'll be cached.

Also updated `workshop_update.vdf` with the v0.2.2 changenote.

Want me to change the username in the task or use a different authentication method?

why cant i copy and paste my passowrd into termianal

---

_**Agent (model copilot/claude-opus-4.5)**_

The user is having trouble pasting their password into the terminal when running SteamCMD. This is a common issue with password prompts in terminals - they often don't show the characters being typed (for security) and sometimes don't accept paste.

A few things could be happening:
1. The password field is in "secure input" mode and doesn't display anything when typing/pasting
2. Right-click paste might work instead of Ctrl+V
3. PowerShell terminal has different paste behavior

Let me give them some tips.In PowerShell/terminal, password prompts often have these quirks:

1. **Right-click to paste** - In Windows Terminal/PowerShell, right-click pastes instead of Ctrl+V

2. **Nothing shows when typing** - Password fields hide input for security (no asterisks or characters appear, but it IS accepting input)

3. **Try typing it** - If paste isn't working, type the password manually (it won't show, but it's being entered)

4. **Alternative**: Run SteamCMD with password inline (less secure but works):
   ```
   C:\steamcmd\steamcmd.exe +login FlorpyDorp "YourPasswordHere" +quit
   ```

If you're in VS Code's integrated terminal, try opening a regular Windows Terminal or Command Prompt instead - they handle interactive input better.

The user is asking about questions from another developer who wants to contribute to the descriptions.json file. Let me answer these questions:

1. deviceKey discovery workflow - How to find the page keys
2. Rate limits on tooltip length - Any limits on text
3. Schema file - Would help with validation

Let me check the codebase to provide accurate answers.Great questions! Let me check the codebase to give accurate answers: Now let me check if there's an existing command to dump keys: Now I have enough info. Here are the answers for your collaborator:

---

**Answers for your collaborator:**

### 1. DeviceKey Discovery Workflow

Currently there's no "dump all keys" command built-in. The deviceKey is usually:
- The **PrefabName** of the item (e.g., `StructureArcFurnace`, `ItemKitPipe`)
- Visible in-game when you hover over the Prefab Name field (our new tooltip shows it!)

**I can add a dump command** - want me to add a console command like `spda_dumpkeys` that outputs all page keys to a file?

Quick workaround for now - they can access `Stationpedia.StationpediaPages` which is a `List<StationpediaPage>` where each has a `.Key` property.

### 2. Tooltip Length Limits

**No hard limits enforced by our code**, but practical considerations:
- Unity's TextMeshPro handles long strings fine, but very long tooltips (1000+ chars) may:
  - Extend off-screen (we don't have auto-scrolling tooltips)
  - Cause minor performance hiccups on first render
- **Recommended**: Keep descriptions under ~500 characters, use `\n` for line breaks
- Rich text tags (`<color>`, `<b>`, etc.) are supported and don't count toward visual length

### 3. Schema File - YES!

Great idea. I can create a JSON Schema file (`descriptions.schema.json`) that:
- Validates structure
- Provides autocomplete in VS Code
- Documents all valid keys
- Shows required vs optional fields

---

**Want me to:**
1. Add a `spda_dumpkeys` console command that exports all page keys to a file?
2. Create a JSON Schema file for descriptions.json?

static string DumpPageKeysCommand(string[] args)\r\n        {\r\n            try\r\n            {\r\n                var pages = Stationpedia.StationpediaPages;\r\n                if (pages == null || pages.Count == 0)\r\n                {\r\n                    return \"No Stationpedia pages found. Open Stationpedia first.\";\r\n                }\r\n                \r\n                // Build output\r\n                var sb = new System.Text.StringBuilder();\r\n                sb.AppendLine(\"# Stationpedia Page Keys\");\r\n                sb.AppendLine($\"# Generated: {DateTime.Now:yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss}\");\r\n                sb.AppendLine($\"# Total pages: {pages.Count}\");\r\n                sb.AppendLine(\"#\");\r\n                sb.AppendLine(\"# Use these keys as 'deviceKey' in descriptions.json\");\r\n                sb.AppendLine(\"# Format: Key | Title | Category\");\r\n                sb.AppendLine(\"#\");\r\n                sb.AppendLine();\r\n                \r\n                // Group by category\r\n                var byCategory = new Dictionary&lt;string, List&lt;StationpediaPage&gt;&gt;();\r\n                foreach (var page in pages)\r\n                {\r\n                    string cat = page.CategoryString ?? \"Uncategorized\";\r\n                    if (!byCategory.ContainsKey(cat))\r\n                        byCategory[cat] = new List&lt;StationpediaPage&gt;();\r\n                    byCategory[cat].Add(page);\r\n                }\r\n                \r\n                foreach (var kvp in byCategory)\r\n                {\r\n                    sb.AppendLine($\"## {kvp.Key}\");\r\n                    sb.AppendLine();\r\n                    foreach (var page in kvp.Value)\r\n                    {\r\n                        sb.AppendLine($\"{page.Key} | {page.Title}\");\r\n                    }\r\n                    sb.AppendLine();\r\n                }\r\n                \r\n                // Save to file\r\n                string outputPath = System.IO.Path.Combine(\r\n                    Environment.GetFolderPath(Environment.SpecialFolder.MyDocuments),\r\n                    \"My Games\", \"Stationeers\", \"stationpedia_keys.txt\");\r\n                \r\n                System.IO.File.WriteAllText(outputPath, sb.ToString());\r\n                \r\n                return $\"Exported {pages.Count} page keys to:\\n{outputPath}\";\r\n            }\r\n            catch (Exception ex)\r\n            {\r\n                return $\"Error dumping keys\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n",

Open Stationpedia first.\";\r\n                }\r\n                \r\n                // Build output\r\n                var sb = new System.Text.StringBuilder();\r\n                sb.AppendLine(\"# Stationpedia Page Keys\");\r\n                sb.AppendLine($\"# Generated: {DateTime.Now:yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss}\");\r\n                sb.AppendLine($\"# Total pages: {pages.Count}\");\r\n                sb.AppendLine(\"#\");\r\n                sb.AppendLine(\"# Use these keys as 'deviceKey' in descriptions.json\");\r\n                sb.AppendLine(\"# Format: Key | Title | Category\");\r\n                sb.AppendLine(\"#\");\r\n                sb.AppendLine();\r\n                \r\n                // Group by category\r\n                var byCategory = new Dictionary&lt;string, List&lt;StationpediaPage&gt;&gt;();\r\n                foreach (var page in pages)\r\n                {\r\n                    string cat = page.CategoryString ?? \"Uncategorized\";\r\n                    if (!byCategory.ContainsKey(cat))\r\n                        byCategory[cat] = new List&lt;StationpediaPage&gt;();\r\n                    byCategory[cat].Add(page);\r\n                }\r\n                \r\n                foreach (var kvp in byCategory)\r\n                {\r\n                    sb.AppendLine($\"## {kvp.Key}\");\r\n                    sb.AppendLine();\r\n                    foreach (var page in kvp.Value)\r\n                    {\r\n                        sb.AppendLine($\"{page.Key} | {page.Title}\");\r\n                    }\r\n                    sb.AppendLine();\r\n                }\r\n                \r\n                // Save to file\r\n                string outputPath = System.IO.Path.Combine(\r\n                    Environment.GetFolderPath(Environment.SpecialFolder.MyDocuments),\r\n                    \"My Games\", \"Stationeers\", \"stationpedia_keys.txt\");\r\n                \r\n                System.IO.File.WriteAllText(outputPath, sb.ToString());\r\n                \r\n                return $\"Exported {pages.Count} page keys to:\\n{outputPath}\";\r\n            }\r\n            catch (Exception ex)\r\n            {\r\n                return $\"Error dumping keys",
```json
  "toolCallResult": null
}
```

The user wants to understand what page keys are and why they're needed, and they want me to create a JSON schema file for descriptions.json.

Let me explain page keys first, then create the schema.

Page keys are the unique identifiers used by the game's Stationpedia system to identify each page. They're typically the PrefabName of items/structures (like "StructureArcFurnace", "ItemKitPipe") or special keys for category pages. The mod uses these keys to match custom descriptions/tooltips to the correct Stationpedia pages.

Now let me create a JSON schema file.**Page Keys** are the unique identifiers Stationpedia uses internally to identify each page. They're needed because:

1. **Matching content to pages** - When you open "Arc Furnace" in Stationpedia, the game loads the page with key `StructureArcFurnace`. Our mod looks up that key in `descriptions.json` to find your custom content.

2. **They're usually the PrefabName** - For items/structures, the key equals the prefab name (what IC10 uses). Examples:
   - `StructureArcFurnace` → Arc Furnace page
   - `ItemKitPipe` → Pipe Kit page  
   - `ItemGasCanisterOxygen` → Oxygen Canister page

3. **Some special keys exist** for category/info pages like `Home`, `Gases`, `Reagents`, etc.

Now let me create the schema file: Now let me add the schema reference to the descriptions.json file so VS Code picks it up automatically: Now let me also add a `spda_dumpkeys` console command to help discover page keys: Let me check what properties are available: No CategoryString. Let me just simplify the dump command to not group by category: Done! Here's what I created for your collaborator:

---

## Created for your collaborator:

### 1. **JSON Schema File** (`descriptions.schema.json`)
- Full schema with descriptions for every field
- Enables VS Code autocomplete and validation
- Just add `"$schema": "./descriptions.schema.json"` at top of their `descriptions.json` (already added to yours)

### 2. **Console Commands** (in-game)

| Command | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| `spda_dumpkeys` | Exports ALL page keys to `Documents/My Games/Stationeers/stationpedia_keys.txt` |
| `spda_currentkey` | Shows the deviceKey of the currently viewed page |

### 3. **Tooltip Length Answer**
No hard limit, but recommend **under 500 characters** for best UX. Rich text supported.

---

**Send them these files:**
- `descriptions.schema.json` - for validation/autocomplete
- `CONFIGURATION.md` - documentation (already exists)

They can run `spda_dumpkeys` in-game console to get a list of all ~2000+ page keys sorted alphabetically.

The user is having an issue with the Python editor where they can't click on devices in the search results. Let me examine the Python editor code to see what's wrong.I found the issue. In the `DeviceListItem` creation, the `on_click` lambda is capturing the device variable in a way that may not work correctly due to late binding. Let me check the `components.py` file to see the `DeviceListItem` implementation: Found it! The problem is in `DeviceListItem` - it's passing `self.device_key` (a string) to the `on_click` callback, but in `main.py` the callback expects a `DeviceDescription` object. Let me fix this: Also, let me fix the CategoryTreeItem for consistency: Fixed! The issue was that:

1. **DeviceListItem** was calling `on_click(self.device_key)` - passing the string key instead of letting the lambda in `main.py` handle passing the device object
2. **CategoryTreeItem** had the same issue with `on_click(self.text_value)`

Now both components call `on_click()` with no arguments, which lets the lambda closures in `main.py` properly capture and pass the correct objects (`device` and `category` respectively).

The editor should now work - you'll be able to click on devices in the search results.


The editor uses CustomTkinter for the GUI with the orange Stationeers theme.
